title: Locality Barriers for Spread Regularization
subtitle: Impossibility theorems for linear and product families: the workshop set out to prove an exp(Ω(w log w)) lower bound and ended with a layered no-go instead.
summary: We study whether local-link regularization can force strong spread properties at multiplicative cost $\exp(\Omega(w \log w))$ for $w$-uniform families. The main contribution is negative. First, the original fixed-$C$ target is false as stated: if the regularity parameter satisfies $\kappa(w) = \Theta(\log w)$, then for every fixed $C > 1$ and all sufficiently large $w$, the initial family is already $C$-spread, so the proposed regularization may terminate at time $0$. Second, for $q$-linear transversal kernel families $\mathcal{H}(G, q)$ we derive an exact witness-probability formula $\Pr[S \subseteq A] = q^{-r_N(S)}$ and show linking is exactly contraction in the dual matroid; this caps every link-only regularization at multiplicative loss $\exp(O(w \log \log w))$, far below the desired barrier. Several no-go theorems then rule out scalar-threshold, size-only, single-weighted-witness, and full-symmetrization repairs.

models.label: Codex GPT-5.4
models.role: all three personas (The Builder, The Formalizer, The Adversary)

transcripts.label: Run 1 — adversarial workshop: original target falsified, link-only impossibility proven
